zeroconf_confirm.title: Sunlit battery discovered
zeroconf_confirm.description: A Sunlit BK215 battery (serial {serial_number}) was found on your network. Sunlit reads its data from the Sunlit Solar cloud, so sign in with your Sunlit account on the next screen to set it up.

user.title: Sunlit Authentication
user.description: Enter your credentials to connect to Sunlit Solar API ({api_url})

select_families.title: Select Families
select_families.description: Select one or more families to monitor. Each family represents a group of devices at a specific location.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the Sunlit API
error.no_families: No families found on this account
error.no_selection: Please select at least one family

data_description.enable_soc_events: Enable or disable battery SOC event notifications
data_description.soc_threshold_critical_low: Battery SOC percentage considered critically low
data_description.soc_threshold_critical_high: Battery SOC percentage considered critically high
data_description.soc_change_threshold: Minimum SOC change percentage to trigger an event

import_history.name: Import historical statistics
import_history.description: Backfill historical generation and earnings from the Sunlit / SunEnergyXT cloud into Home Assistant's long-term statistics, so pre-install history shows up in the Energy Dashboard and Statistics cards. Safe to run more than once; existing daily buckets are overwritten rather than duplicated.
